[2018-03-08 10:06] 307th: @Plato i did include a brief explanation of absorb at the end of section 2
[2018-03-08 10:06] 307th: "However, not all your blockers have to die on defense - if you put damage on a blocker that isn't enough to kill it, it instantly regenerates (as long as it isn't Fragile). This is called absorbing damage, and is the subject of the next section."
[2018-03-08 10:14] steel0229e (Steel): I don't understand why vivid drone has such a big p1 winrate according to prismata-stats. Are there any good p1 vivid lines I am missing?
[2018-03-08 10:14] 307th: wouldn't be surprised if the old vivid drone is having an effect there
[2018-03-08 10:15] 307th: also people tend to mess up as p2 in high econ sets
[2018-03-08 10:15] 307th: the correct line for p2 in high econ sets is 1EE 1DD, but mostly people will go DEE 1DD or DD DDEE
[2018-03-08 10:16] 307th: both of those two lines end up being quite a bit worse
[2018-03-08 10:22] crash_overlord: Thanks for letting me know
[2018-03-08 10:22] crash_overlord: What's a p1 vivid line
[2018-03-08 10:23] 307th: 1EE 11 DDDD is a high econ line for p1, it's not that much better than DD DDE DDD though
[2018-03-08 10:23] 307th: so you can stick to DD DDE most of the time since that's way more flexible
[2018-03-08 10:25] crash_overlord: Ok so how/when do you pickup vivid other than like floating 2 gold/buying shitty engy
[2018-03-08 10:26] crash_overlord: New vivid just feels really weird for me
[2018-03-08 10:26] 307th: it's more efficient than regular drone, so in theory you want your economy to end up being all or nearly-all vivids
[2018-03-08 10:26] 307th: with just a few regular drones for possible forcefielding etc.
[2018-03-08 10:27] crash_overlord: But Is it better enough to warrant buying new engineers for energy?
[2018-03-08 10:27] 307th: it can be tricky since it costs 2 energy, so if you want to increase your economy by a lot still, you can either buy regular drones, or buy vivid and make new engineers
[2018-03-08 10:28] 307th: which one you go for should depend on how close you are to your ideal econ size
[2018-03-08 10:28] 307th: which is why 1EE is best for both players if they're confident they're going for a high econ build
[2018-03-08 10:28] 307th: if you just want to increase your economy by a medium amount you can make two drones and then add on a vivid/turn for a while
[2018-03-08 10:29] 307th: an engineer to buy vivids pays for itself pretty quickly though
[2018-03-08 10:29] 307th: it essentially makes 1 gold per turn for every turn you make a vivid with it
[2018-03-08 10:29] 307th: so after two turns you're strictly up an engineer compared to someone staying on regular drones (although more of your income is in vivids)

--- Thread (19 messages, 2018-03-08) ---
[2018-03-08 12:42] mtanzer: isn't DD DDEE committing a bit less?
[2018-03-08 12:44] 307th: both of them end up on 11 economy at the end of turn 2
[2018-03-08 12:45] 307th: the difference being the 1EE 1DD line floats 1 gold, while the DD DDEE line floats 0
[2018-03-08 12:45] 307th: because of this, the 1EE 1DD line has 12 gold on turn 3, while the DD DDEE line has 11
[2018-03-08 12:45] 307th: this means that the 1EE 1DD line can go DDDD or 1DDC, while the DD DDEE line cannot do either of those
[2018-03-08 12:46] 307th: so, it's true the DD DDEE line is less committal on turn 1 (so is less vulnerable to e.g. elyot animus), but it falls way behind on turn 3
[2018-03-08 12:46] 307th: because that 1 gold disadvantage ends up making a big difference
[2018-03-08 12:46] mtanzer: hmm I never actually studied vivid openings but I did write a program a few years ago when the current version was new
[2018-03-08 12:47] mtanzer: and I thought it found that the 1EE and DD DDE openings transposed after like 4 or 5 turns
[2018-03-08 12:47] mtanzer: granted maybe you usually want tech before that and maybe the 1EE openings end up ahead in that case?
[2018-03-08 12:48] 307th: they don't transpose, unless the DD DDEE player can catch up by going up to 5-6 engineers and turning all their drones into vivids
